category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Writing your first Django app, part 1
surface.selection_basis: The Django 6.0 documentation places the quick install guide immediately before its official first-app tutorial. Part 1 is a bounded guide that ends by verifying the first polls view in a browser and then directs the reader to part 2.

alternatives_considered.name: Quick install guide
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is required setup and ends by directing the reader to the tutorial; it does not create or run an application.

alternatives_considered.name: Django at a glance
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is an architectural overview rather than the named first-app tutorial.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install Django, create the tutorial project and polls app, and serve the first view locally.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Django.
The developer has a computer, shell, browser, and permission to install Python packages and create local files.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A locally running Django 6.0 development server routes /polls/ to the tutorial's index view.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The browser at http://localhost:8000/polls/ displays: Hello, world. You're at the polls index.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Part 1 explicitly says to verify the wired index view in the browser, gives the expected text, and then sends the reader to part 2.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the final verification in the official part 1 journey. The earlier rocket page verifies only the project scaffold; the final polls response verifies an application, view, URL configuration, server, and browser request together.

prerequisites.requirement: Python 3.12 or later for the Django 6.0 tutorial, plus a shell and browser.
Django 6.0 installed in the selected Python environment; the official release is the recommended installation choice for most users.
Use documentation matching the installed Django version; switch tutorial versions or upgrade if the installed version differs.

primary_path.action: Install the latest compatible Python if it is not already available, then verify it from the shell.
details: SQLite ships with Python, so no external database is needed for this first path.
primary_path.input: A supported operating system and package installation access
primary_path.success_signal: The Python shell starts and prints its version.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An older Python version requires either a compatible older Django documentation version or a Python upgrade.

primary_path.action: Install an official Django release in the Python environment.
details: The quick install guide recommends an official release for most users.
Operating-system packages and the development version are documented alternatives.
primary_path.success_signal: Python can import Django and print its version.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package installation or import errors block the tutorial.

primary_path.action: Run python -m django --version, or py -m django --version on Windows, and confirm the result matches the Django 6.0 tutorial.
details: No module named django means Django is not installed in that Python environment.
primary_path.success_signal: The command prints a Django version compatible with the selected documentation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the version differs, use the version switcher or update Django before continuing.

primary_path.action: Change to the intended parent directory and run django-admin startproject mysite djangotutorial.
details: Do not use names such as django or test because they conflict with built-in packages.
primary_path.output: djangotutorial directory containing manage.py and the mysite package
primary_path.success_signal: The documented project file tree is created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If django-admin fails, use the linked troubleshooting guidance before continuing.

primary_path.action: Change into djangotutorial and run python manage.py runserver, or py manage.py runserver on Windows.
details: Ignore the initial unapplied-migrations warning for this part of the tutorial.
The development server is only for development, not production.
primary_path.success_signal: System checks report no issues and the server start address is printed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A port or environment error must be resolved; adding files later can require restarting because not every new file triggers automatic reload.

details: This is the intermediate project-scaffold verification.
primary_path.success_signal: A Congratulations page with a rocket appears and states that it worked.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No specific retry is documented beyond checking the running server and URL.

primary_path.action: From the directory containing manage.py, run python manage.py startapp polls, or the Windows py equivalent.
details: The command scaffolds the polls Python package with views.py and other app files.
primary_path.success_signal: The documented polls file tree exists.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If a newly added file is not detected, restart runserver as the guide notes.

primary_path.action: Replace polls/views.py with the documented HttpResponse index view.
details: Import HttpResponse and return the exact tutorial greeting from index(request).
primary_path.success_signal: views.py defines index and its response text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Python syntax or import errors prevent the server from loading the view.

primary_path.action: Create polls/urls.py and map the empty app route to views.index with the name index.
details: Import path from django.urls and views from the current app.
primary_path.success_signal: polls/urls.py contains the documented urlpatterns list.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing file, import, or route prevents URL resolution.

primary_path.action: Update mysite/urls.py to import include and mount polls.urls at polls/.
details: Keep the existing admin route and add path('polls/', include('polls.urls')).
primary_path.input: Project URL configuration and polls URL configuration
primary_path.output: Root route connected to the polls app
primary_path.success_signal: The root urlpatterns list contains both polls and admin routes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing include import or incorrect module string causes startup or routing errors.

primary_path.action: Ensure runserver is running, then open http://localhost:8000/polls/ in the browser.
details: If the server stopped or did not reload after new files were added, run the documented runserver command again.
primary_path.input: Running server and wired polls route
primary_path.output: HTTP response from the first Django view
primary_path.success_signal: The browser shows: Hello, world. You're at the polls index.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If a page-not-found response appears, confirm the URL includes /polls/ rather than opening only the site root.

branches.condition: The developer uses an older supported Python release.
branches.path: Select the Django version and matching documentation compatible with that Python version instead of following the 6.0 route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The same conceptual journey applies, but commands or generated code may differ by documentation version.

branches.condition: The developer does not choose the recommended official release.
branches.path: Install a distribution-provided package or the development version and use matching documentation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds platform-specific installation behavior or development-version compatibility risk.

branches.path: Open http://localhost:8000/polls/ exactly, not only http://localhost:8000/, and retry.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Corrects the requested route without changing the app.

friction_gates.description: Django 6.0 requires Python 3.12 or later and version-matched documentation.
Python and Django must be installed locally before the first-app tutorial can begin.
A local TCP port and long-running development-server process must remain available through verification.
The developer must create and connect a view, an app URLconf, and the project URLconf before the first application response appears.

sources.title: Writing your first Django app, part 1
evidence_supported: Complete ordered project and app path

excluded_after_success: Database migration and model setup in part 2
